title: FR-Match: cell type matching for scRNAseq data
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"488uc","text":"FR-Match is a supervised cell phenotype matching strategy for cluster-to-cluster cell transcriptome integration across scRNAseq experiments.  ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8d48f","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1v3mg","text":"An R package and Shiny application are provided at https://github.com/JCVenterInstitute/FRmatch.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":51,"length":44}],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"E72695B2046F11EBB7D70A58A9FEAC2A","url":"https://github.com/JCVenterInstitute/FRmatch"}}}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"9g2vi","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"eqsg6","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":[]},{"key":"421i7","text":"Require R Shiny package.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"software","mutability":"","data":{"id":668,"guid":"49F7F2CD341F11EE9D4302C0B41BC903","name":"R programming language","version":"3.3.3 of later","os_name":"","os_version":"","developer":"The R Foundation","repository":"Comprehensive R Archive Network","link":"https://cran.r-project.org/","is_public":true,"can_edit":false}}}}
steps: Use the built-in data preparation function to create data objects with required and optional input data elements. Create data objects for experiment 1 (E1) and experiment 2 (E2) dat_E1
View comparative cell cluster sizes. plot_clusterSize(dat_E1, dat_E2)
View "barcode" plot for cluster of interest. plot_cluster_by_markers(dat_E1, cluster.name = "cluster_of_interest")
Use wrapper function to perform bi-directional matching.
Combine the bi-directional matching results and plot. plot_bi_FRmatch(rst12, rst21)
Some optional plotting functions to help studying the matching results.
Plot one-directional matching results. plot_FRmatch(rst12) Plot one-directional matching p-values. plot_FRmatch(rst12, type = "padj")
Minimum spanning tree (MST) plot. MST can be plotted by turning on the plot option in the test function. FR.test(samp1, samp2, plot.MST = TRUE)
Interactively explore and match scRNAseq cell type clusters with the seamless Shiny app. The Shiny app may serve as a quick start demo with pre-loaded datasets. runShiny()
creator: Yun Renee Zhang
